channel.title: NPR Topics: Business
channel.description: Find the latest business news with reports on Wall Street, interest rates, banking, companies, and U.S. and world financial markets. Subscribe to the Business Story of the Day podcast.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: The hidden power keeping wages low
item.description: For decades, economists gave short shrift to the idea of monopsony — a power employers can have to suppress wages. Now a wave of research suggests it's everywhere, and a new book argues it's key to understanding today's inequality.
item.content:encoded: For decades, economists gave short shrift to the idea of monopsony — a power employers can have to suppress wages. Now a wave of research suggests it's everywhere, and a new book argues it's key to understanding today's inequality.
item.dc:creator: Greg Rosalsky

item.title: Here are 3 takeaways as Trump's pick to lead the Fed faces a confirmation fight
item.description: President Trump's pick to lead the Federal Reserve went before a Senate committee today — but Kevin Warsh's confirmation could be held up by forces that are outside his control.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump's pick to lead the Federal Reserve went before a Senate committee today — but Kevin Warsh's confirmation could be held up by forces that are outside his control.

(Image credit: Tierney L. Cross)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: She raised concerns about her company's contracts with ICE. Then she lost her job
item.description: Billie Little had worked for Thomson Reuters for about two decades. She was fired after questioning whether federal immigration agents unlawfully used their products.
item.content:encoded:
Billie Little had worked for Thomson Reuters for about two decades. She was fired after questioning whether federal immigration agents unlawfully used their products.

(Image credit: Octavio Jones)
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block

item.title: Tim Cook to step down as Apple CEO
item.description: Tim Cook announced he will step down as Apple's CEO in September, becoming the company's executive chairman. The new CEO will be John Ternus, currently Apple's senior VP of hardware engineering.
item.content:encoded: Tim Cook announced he will step down as Apple's CEO in September, becoming the company's executive chairman. The new CEO will be John Ternus, currently Apple's senior VP of hardware engineering.
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: The Onion has agreed to a new deal to take over Infowars
item.description: The Onion says it has a new deal to take over conspiracy theorist Alex Jones' Infowars media company. If approved, the satirical news website could turn Infowars into a parody of itself.
item.content:encoded:
The Onion says it has a new deal to take over conspiracy theorist Alex Jones' Infowars media company. If approved, the satirical news website could turn Infowars into a parody of itself.

(Image credit: Mario Tama)
item.dc:creator: Tovia Smith

item.title: Trump's labor secretary resigns amid investigation into misconduct
item.description: Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer, who was under internal investigation, is leaving her position. She becomes the third cabinet departure of President Trump's second term.
item.content:encoded:
Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer, who was under internal investigation, is leaving her position. She becomes the third cabinet departure of President Trump's second term.

(Image credit: Drew Angerer)
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu

item.title: In the American Heartland, farmers are literally betting the farm to stay in business
item.description: American farmers hope the Iran ceasefire holds and fertilizer and diesel start moving through the Strait of Hormuz soon, as row crop farmers go into their fourth straight planting season in the red.
item.content:encoded: American farmers hope the Iran ceasefire holds and fertilizer and diesel start moving through the Strait of Hormuz soon, as row crop farmers go into their fourth straight planting season in the red.
item.dc:creator: Kirk Siegler

item.title: How Saudi Arabia's entertainment investments are playing out at home and abroad
item.description: NPR's Scott Detrow talks with Scott Roxborough of The Hollywood Reporter about the meaning of Saudi Arabia's investment in the potential Paramount Skydance/Warner Brothers Discovery merger.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Detrow talks with Scott Roxborough of The Hollywood Reporter about the meaning of Saudi Arabia's investment in the potential Paramount Skydance/Warner Brothers Discovery merger.
item.dc:creator: Marc Rivers

item.title: Small business owners queue up for tariff refunds
item.description: Business people had their fingers above the keys, ready to go, when the U.S. government launched its tariff-refund portal Monday morning.
item.content:encoded: Business people had their fingers above the keys, ready to go, when the U.S. government launched its tariff-refund portal Monday morning.
item.dc:creator: Alina Selyukh

item.title: CEO of Minnesota-based company shares her experience applying for a tariff refund
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with the chief executive of a Minnesota baby products manufacturer about her experience applying for a refund from President Trump's tariffs.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with the chief executive of a Minnesota baby products manufacturer about her experience applying for a refund from President Trump's tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin
